struct Fragment
  n1 : Int64
  n0 : Int64
  length : Int64
end

def power(base : Int64, exp : Int, mod : Int64) : Int64
  res = 1_i64
  base = base % mod
  while exp > 0
    if exp % 2 == 1
      res = ((res * base) % mod)
    end
    base = ((base * base) % mod)
    exp /= 2
  end
  res
end

def solve(nums1 : Array(Int64), nums0 : Array(Int64)) : Int64
  MOD = 1_000_000_007_i64

  fragments = Array(Fragment)
  totalLength = 0_i64

  for i in 0..(nums1.size - 1)
    n1 = nums1[i]
    n0 = nums0[i]
    length = n1 + n0
    fragments.push(Fragment(n1, n0, length))
    totalLength += length
  end

  # Sort fragments based on the greedy rule:
  # A precedes B if (n1A > n1B) OR (n1A == n1B AND n0A < n0B)
  fragments.sort do |a, b|
    if a.n1 != b.n1
      # Descending n1
      b.n1 <=> a.n1
    else
      # Ascending n0
      a.n0 <=> b.n0
    end
  end

  totalValue = 0_i64
  lRemaining = totalLength

  for frag in fragments
    n1 = frag.n1
    n0 = frag.n0
    length = frag.length

    # L_remaining now holds the length of the suffix (all fragments after current one)
    lRemaining -= length

    # 1. Calculate V_i = (2^n1 - 1) * 2^n0 mod MOD
    
    # P1 = 2^n1 mod MOD
    p1 = power(2_i64, n1.to_i, MOD)
    
    # V1 = (2^n1 - 1) mod MOD
    # Add MOD before modulo to ensure positive result for subtraction
    v1 = (p1 - 1_i64 + MOD) % MOD
    
    # P0 = 2^n0 mod MOD
    p0 = power(2_i64, n0.to_i, MOD)
    
    # V_i = V1 * P0 mod MOD
    vi = ((v1 * p0) % MOD)

    # 2. Calculate contribution: V_i * 2^L_remaining mod MOD
    
    # L_remaining is the exponent. Since total length <= 2*10^5, it fits in Int.
    powerOf2 = power(2_i64, lRemaining.to_i, MOD)
    contribution = ((vi * powerOf2) % MOD)

    # 3. Update total value
    totalValue = (totalValue + contribution) % MOD
  end

  totalValue
end

struct Fragment
  n1 : Int64
  n0 : Int64
  length : Int64
end

def power(base : Int64, exp : Int, mod : Int64) : Int64
  res = 1_i64
  base = base % mod
  while exp > 0
    if exp % 2 == 1
      res = ((res * base) % mod)
    end
    base = ((base * base) % mod)
    exp /= 2
  end
  res
end

def solve(nums1 : Array(Int64), nums0 : Array(Int64)) : Int64
  MOD = 1_000_000_007_i64

  fragments = Array(Fragment)
  totalLength = 0_i64

  for i in 0..(nums1.size - 1)
    n1 = nums1[i]
    n0 = nums0[i]
    length = n1 + n0
    fragments.push(Fragment(n1, n0, length))
    totalLength += length
  end

  # Sort fragments based on the greedy rule:
  # A precedes B if (n1A > n1B) OR (n1A == n1B AND n0A < n0B)
  fragments.sort do |a, b|
    if a.n1 != b.n1
      # Descending n1
      b.n1 <=> a.n1
    else
      # Ascending n0
      a.n0 <=> b.n0
    end
  end

  totalValue = 0_i64
  lRemaining = totalLength

  for frag in fragments
    n1 = frag.n1
    n0 = frag.n0
    length = frag.length

    # L_remaining now holds the length of the suffix (all fragments after current one)
    lRemaining -= length

    # 1. Calculate V_i = (2^n1 - 1) * 2^n0 mod MOD
    
    # P1 = 2^n1 mod MOD
    p1 = power(2_i64, n1.to_i, MOD)
    
    # V1 = (2^n1 - 1) mod MOD
    # Add MOD before modulo to ensure positive result for subtraction
    v1 = (p1 - 1_i64 + MOD) % MOD
    
    # P0 = 2^n0 mod MOD
    p0 = power(2_i64, n0.to_i, MOD)
    
    # V_i = V1 * P0 mod MOD
    vi = ((v1 * p0) % MOD)

    # 2. Calculate contribution: V_i * 2^L_remaining mod MOD
    
    # L_remaining is the exponent. Since total length <= 2*10^5, it fits in Int.
    powerOf2 = power(2_i64, lRemaining.to_i, MOD)
    contribution = ((vi * powerOf2) % MOD)

    # 3. Update total value
    totalValue = (totalValue + contribution) % MOD
  end

  totalValue
end
```
